issues.current_text: `Some` wrapping the constant value if `T` is a constant singleton type, or `None` otherwise
issues.problem: The existing description in the doc body says it returns `None` 'if a constant value cannot be constructed from the provided type', which is slightly broader than 'is a constant singleton type'. The return tag could more closely match the body's framing.
issues.suggestion: `Some` wrapping the constant value if one can be constructed from `T`, or `None` otherwise

issues.current_text: a placeholder value of type `T` usable only in an inline match; the value is never actually evaluated at runtime
issues.problem: Minor wording: 'never actually evaluated at runtime' is slightly redundant — 'never evaluated at runtime' reads more cleanly. Also, calling it a 'placeholder value' is fine but could be more precise since the method body is infinite recursion and any actual invocation would not terminate.
issues.suggestion: a value of type `T` usable only as the scrutinee of an inline match; it is never evaluated at runtime

general_feedback: The filled-in @param, @tparam, and @return tags are accurate, clear, and consistent with the surrounding Scaladoc style (lowercase start, backticks around type references, concise single-sentence form). All return descriptions correctly reflect what the methods produce. Only minor stylistic suggestions; no errors or warnings.
